fields.title: Child Safe Standards - Foundation
fields.description: Mandatory training covering Victorias Child Safe Standards framework, reporting obligations, and risk identification.

fields.title: First Aid & CPR Refresher
fields.description: Annual first aid refresher including CPR technique updates and emergency response procedures for OSHC settings.

fields.title: Behaviour Guidance Strategies
fields.description: Positive behaviour support techniques, de-escalation strategies, and documentation requirements.

fields.title: Data Privacy & Confidentiality
fields.description: Staff obligations under the Privacy Act, handling of family and child records, and incident reporting protocols.

fields.title: Child Protection Awareness
fields.description: Mandatory training for all staff on child protection policies.

fields.title: Recognising Signs of Abuse or Neglect
Emergency Response at an OSHC Service
Introduction to frame work
fields.content_url:
The name comes from the musical instrument, which expands and contracts to produce soundùsimilarly, these UI sections "expand" to show details and "collapse" to hide them
Mobbin
Mobbin
How it Works
Header: A visible title or label that acts as a control.
Panel: The hidden content (text, images, or forms) that appears when the header is activated.
Indicator Icon: Usually a plus/minus sign or a chevron (arrow) that changes direction based on whether the section is open or closed. 
Nielsen Norman Group
Nielsen Norman Group
 +4
Key Benefits
Saves Space: It allows large amounts of information to be stored in a compact area, which is especially useful for mobile screens.
Reduces Clutter: By hiding non-essential details, it prevents users from feeling overwhelmed and lowers "cognitive load".
Improves Scannability: Users can quickly scan the headers to find the specific topic they need without scrolling through long pages.

fields.question_text: What should you do if you suspect a child is being abused?
How many Child Safe Standards are there in Victoria?
Educators are mandatory reporters under the Children, Youth and Families Act 2005.
Which of the following is NOT one of the Child Safe Standards?
Describe in your own words what "child safe culture" means and give one example of how it can be demonstrated in a childcare setting.
What steps would you take if a child disclosed abuse to you? List at least 3 steps.
What is the correct compression-to-breath ratio for adult CPR?
You should check for danger before starting CPR.
How long should you continue CPR before stopping?
Which of the following is a sign that CPR is effective?
When should you use an AED (defibrillator)?
Under the Privacy Act, staff may share child records with parents on request without restriction.
Which of the following is a correct practice when handling child records?

fields.option_text: Report it immediately to your supervisor and document it
Ignore it as it is not your responsibility
Talk to the child directly about the abuse
Wait and observe before taking any action
Environments and activities are child safe
Complaints and concerns are taken seriously
Until the person shows signs of life or help arrives
As soon as one is available for an unresponsive person with no pulse
Only when the person is conscious
Only trained doctors can use an AED
